Notice Of Second Motion And Second Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Further Responses To Defendant'S Supplemental Interrogatory Responses (Set 1), Specifically Special Interrogatory 4, Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Or, In The Alternative To Compelling Further Responses, (2) Request For Evidentiary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,939.56 Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Jointly And Severally Against Defendant And Its Counsel
Off Calendar Jun-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CARRIE MYLES' Second Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Further Responses To Defendant'S Supplemental Interrogatory Responses (Set 1), Specifically Special Interrogatory 4, Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Or, In The Alternative To Compelling Further Responses, (2) Request For Evidentiary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,939.56 Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Jointly And Severally Against Defendant And Its Counsel.

Plaintiff Carrie Myules' second motion for further responses to special interrogatory 4 and for evidentiary and monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety.

Defendant Urban Alchemy's request for sanctions is also denied. The portion of the motion seeking to compel further responses and for evidentiary sanctions is denied as moot since Urban Alchemy has fully responded to interrogatory 4 and also produced all of Ms. Wear's notes of her investigation. Both sides' requests for monetary sanctions are denied since both sides acted with substantial justification as to the matters relating to this motion.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/HEK)

Lynn Hollenbeck, Esq., / Jasmyne Shaw, Esq., FREEMAN MATHIS & GARY, LLP 415.394.9500 lynn.hollenbeck@fmglaw.com / Jasmyne.shaw@fmglaw.com Attorneys for Defendant URBAN ALCHEMY

Hearing is held. Not ARGUED. Counsel for Defendant seeks clarification of the Court's tentative ruling. Clarification is provided by Court. Court denies defendnats' counsel's oral request to lift sanctions.

PLAINTIFF CARRIE MYLES'S Motion For An Order (1) Compelling Further Responses To Defendant'S Supplemental Special Interrogatory Responses (Set 1), Specifically Interrogatories 2 And 4, Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300 Or, In The Alternative To Compelling Further Responses, (2) Request For Evidentiary Sanctions Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300; And (3) Request For Monetary Sanctions For Attorney Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $5,600.00 Pursuant To C.C.P Section 2030.300

Plaintiff Carris Myles' motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 2 and 4 and for evidentiary and monetary sanctions is denied as moot as to interrogatory 2, granted as to interrogatory 4, denied as to evidentiary sanctions, and granted as to monetary sanctions in the amount of $3,000 against defendant Urban Alchemy. Defendant must provide verified further responses by March 7, 2025 and pay the monetary sanctions no later than March 17, 2025.

The parties agree that defendant provided the contact information of all interviewees in supplemental verified responses served on January 28, 2025, a week after this motion was filed, which renders the motion as to interrogatory 2 moot.

Defendant has taken an impermissibly narrow approach in responding to interrogatory 4. Fairly construed, that interrogatory asks for all information provided by all of the interviewees to Ms. Wear, whether provided orally or in writing and whether or not included in Ms.Wear's report. This requires the defendant to determine if Ms. Wear or any of the interviewees under its control have any notes of what was told Ms. Wear and to ask Ms. Wear and any interviewees under the control of the defendant for their recollections of what was told to Ms. Wear. Defendant's objections to interrogatory 4 and its attempt to narrowly construe the interrogatory lack merit.

Plaintiff has not shown that evidentiary sanctions are warranted, at least unless and until plaintiff receives the further responses to interrogatory 4.

Defendant's responses to interrogatories 2 and 4 lacked substantial justification, necessitating the bringing of this motion. $3,000 is a reasonable amount of fees for preparing the paperwork on a simple motion such as this.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
